cheyenne.meetingHighlights: Capital-city meetings with strong evening and lunchtime options; active newcomer programs.
cheyenne.transitInfo: Cheyenne Transit and regional highways (I-25) provide access; most attendees drive locally.

casper.meetingHighlights: Active local fellowship with steady evening meetings and periodic speaker events.
casper.transitInfo: Local bus services and regional highways; Casper–Natrona County International Airport for regional flights.

laramie.meetingHighlights: University-linked meetings with flexible schedules for students and staff; strong lunchtime attendance.
laramie.transitInfo: Local bus service and drive access via I-80; Amtrak and regional services nearby.

gillette.meetingHighlights: Evening and weekend meetings with strong community support; mining-industry population participation.
gillette.transitInfo: Regional highway access and local bus services; most attendees drive to meetings.

rock-springs.meetingHighlights: Community-focused meetings with steady weekend attendance and occasional speaker nights.
rock-springs.transitInfo: Local transit and regional highways; small regional airport and drive access to larger hubs.

sheridan.neighborhoods: Downtown, Historic Main, South Sheridan, Trail End area
sheridan.meetingHighlights: North-central Wyoming service hub well placed for meetings serving ranching communities and I-90 travelers.
sheridan.transitInfo: Primarily car-based via I-90 and US 87; Sheridan County Airport provides regional flights.

cody.neighborhoods: Downtown, Yellowstone Avenue corridor, Southfork area, Cody Commons
cody.meetingHighlights: Tourism and service center east of Yellowstone, positioned to serve the Bighorn Basin with year-round local demand.
cody.transitInfo: Travel is mostly by car via US 14/16/20; Yellowstone Regional Airport serves the city.

jackson.neighborhoods: Town Square, West Jackson, East Jackson, South Park
jackson.meetingHighlights: Jackson Hole population center with a large service workforce and transit access, supporting varied local meeting needs.
jackson.transitInfo: START buses, pathways, and US 26/89/191 support access; Jackson Hole Airport serves the valley.
